title: Authentic Thai Tom Kha Gai
original_instructions: In a large saucepan or Dutch oven on medium heat, add stock, galangal, lemongrass, and kaffir lime leaves. Bring to a gentle boil. (You are making a quick stock here for the soup base.)
Add chicken and mushroom. Lower heat to medium-high. Cook until the chicken is done (anywhere from 7-8 minutes, depending on your stovetop).
Add the entire can of coconut milk (I used Aroy-D for this recipe). Stir together and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add the cilantro stems and fish sauce, stir, and cook for two more minutes. Taste and adjust the flavors with more fish sauce, lime juice, or sugar. Turn the heat off and serve.
Add lime juice and Thai chilis (if using) and stir in one last time. Turn the heat off and remove the saucepan from the stovetop.
Let cool for a few minutes and portion out in soup bowls for serving. That's it! Simple, easy, healthy, creamy, light, and super delicious!
processed_instructions: Add 4 cups of chicken broth to a large saucepan or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the 3 smashed galangal slices, 3 smashed lemongrass stalks, and 5 kaffir lime leaves to the broth.
Bring the broth to a gentle boil to create a fragrant soup base.
Add 1 pound of bite-sized chicken pieces and 2 cups of sliced mushrooms to the pot.
Lower the heat to medium-high and cook for 7-8 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Pour in the entire can (13.5 ounces) of coconut milk and stir to combine.
Cook the soup for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the coconut milk is fully incorporated.
Add the 1/4 cup of chopped cilantro stems and 3-4 tablespoons of fish sauce to the soup.
Stir and cook for 2 more minutes.
Taste the soup and adjust the flavor with additional fish sauce, lime juice, or a pinch of sugar as needed.
Add 3 tablespoons of lime juice and 2 bird's eye chilis (if using) and stir to combine.
Turn the heat off and remove the saucepan from the stovetop.
Let the soup cool for a few minutes, then ladle into bowls and serve.

- **Ingredient format**: "1.5 inches" corrected to "1 1/2 inches"; "¼ cup" normalized to "1/4 cup" (both are valid fraction formats).
- **Typo fix**: "shitake" corrected to "shiitake" in ingredients.
- **Ingredient cleanup**: Removed conversational asides (e.g., "I used Aroy-D for this recipe"), editorial commentary ("That's it! Simple, easy, healthy..."), and embedded preparation tips from ingredient list reformatted into cleaner descriptors ("lightly smashed" instead of full sentence instructions).

- **isBalanced: False** — Debatable. The dish contains protein (chicken), vegetables (mushrooms), and fats (coconut milk). Could warrant review depending on app's criteria.
- **Gluten-free notes missing**: While `glutenFree: True` is flagged, there is **no note** advising users to verify fish sauce is gluten-free. Many commercial fish sauces contain wheat-based additives or hydrolyzed wheat protein. A substitution note (e.g., "Use certified gluten-free fish sauce such as Red Boat") is needed.

- **isGourmet: False** — Arguable given specialty ingredients like galangal, kaffir lime leaves, and lemongrass, but acceptable depending on app definition.

The agent made substantial and meaningful improvements to quantities in instructions, step granularity, and formatting consistency. However, the missing gluten-free fish sauce note is a significant gap for a recipe flagged as `glutenFree: True`, the cuisineType should be "Thai" not "Asian," and the lime juice sequencing issue could confuse beginners. Adding a note about inedible aromatics would complete the beginner-friendly approach.

title: Beef Stew
original_instructions: Add bacon to a large Dutch oven or stock pot. Turn heat to medium-low and cook, turning flipping every so often, until browned. Remove to a paper towel lined plate and then chop into small pieces.
Pat beef cubes dry with paper towels and add to a ziplock bag with flour, salt and pepper. Toss well to coat.

Heat the pan with bacon grease over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the beef in 2-3 batches (so not to overcrowd the pan), adding a little oil as needed, searing for a few minutes on each side, until browned. Remove meat to a plate and set aside. Reduce heat to medium.

Add onion and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Add vinegar and wine to the pan, cooking over medium-high heat, and scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Add beef broth, bullion, tomato paste, bay leaves, thyme and rosemary. Season with a little more salt and pepper. Add beef and bacon back to the pot. Bring to a boil then reduce heat to a simmer.
Cover and cook for 1 ½ hours or until beef is tender.
Meanwhile, chop the potatoes, carrots, and slice the mushrooms. Set aside.

Cook mushrooms (if using): Add 2 tablespoons butter and 1 tablespoon olive oil to a saute pan over medium high heat. Add mushrooms to the pan in a single layer. Cook for several minutes (without touching). Once golden on the bottom, flip to the other side and cook for a few more minutes.
Add mushrooms, potatoes, carrots and frozen pearl onions to the soup pot and cook for 15-20 more minutes, until carrot and potatoes are tender.
Taste broth. Season as needed with additional salt, pepper, bullion, rosemary, thyme, or some garlic powder. Add a sprinkle of crushed red pepper, if desired.
Ladle some of the hot broth into a bowl and whisk the flour into it, until smooth. Add to pot and cook for 2-5 minutes, until broth has slightly thickened.
Garnish with fresh parsley. Serve with artisan bread.
processed_instructions: Add 8 ounces of chopped bacon to a large Dutch oven or stock pot over medium-low heat.
Cook the bacon, flipping every so often, until browned and crispy.
Remove the bacon to a paper towel-lined plate, chop into small pieces, and set aside. Reserve the bacon grease in the pot.
Pat the 2 1/2 pounds of beef cubes dry with paper towels.
Add the beef cubes to a ziplock bag with 1/3 cup of gluten free flour, salt, and pepper. Toss well to evenly coat all pieces.
Heat the pot with the bacon grease over medium-high heat until hot.
Sear the flour-coated beef in 2-3 batches to avoid overcrowding, adding a little olive oil as needed. Cook for a few minutes on each side until browned on all sides.
Remove the browned beef to a plate and set aside. Reduce the heat to medium.
Add the chopped yellow onion to the pot and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened.
Add the 5 minced garlic cloves and cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Pour in the 2 tablespoons of red wine vinegar and 1 cup of red wine. Increase heat to medium-high.
Scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan with a wooden spoon and simmer the wine mixture for 10 minutes to reduce.

Add 5 cups of beef broth, 2 teaspoons of better than bouillon beef, 1 tablespoon of tomato paste, 2 bay leaves, 1 teaspoon of fresh rosemary (or 1/2 teaspoon dried), and 1 teaspoon of fresh thyme (or 1/2 teaspoon dried) to the pot.
Season with a little more salt and pepper to taste.
Return the seared beef and chopped bacon back to the pot.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to a low simmer.
Cover and cook for 1 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
While the stew simmers, peel and chop the 4 large carrots into diagonal chunks, cut the 1/2 pound of potatoes into small chunks, and slice the 8 ounces of mushrooms. Set aside.
Heat 2 tablespoons of butter and 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a saute pan over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the pan in a single layer and cook for several minutes without stirring until golden on the bottom.
Flip the mushrooms and cook for a few more minutes until golden on the other side. Remove from heat.
Add the sauteed mushrooms, potatoes, carrots, and 8 ounces of frozen pearl onions to the stew pot.
Cook uncovered for 15-20 more minutes, until the carrots and potatoes are fork-tender.
Taste the broth and season as needed with additional salt, pepper, bouillon, rosemary, thyme, or garlic powder.
Ladle some of the hot broth into a small bowl and whisk in 3 tablespoons of gluten free flour until completely smooth with no lumps.
Pour the flour mixture back into the pot and stir to combine.
Cook for 2-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the broth has slightly thickened.
Remove the bay leaves and discard.
Ladle the stew into bowls and garnish with fresh parsley before serving.

title: Chinese Braised Beef Shank
original_instructions: Trim the excess fat off the beef and cut the shank into two to three 1 lb. chunks. Boil a pot of water and boil the beef for about 8 minutes. Drain and rinse the meat.
Put the boiled beef shanks in a small, deep saucepan over medium-low heat and add the wine, 2 kinds of soy sauce, star anise, chili, rock sugar, and cinnamon.
Make sure the meat is all coated in the sauce, rolling it around in the pot for a few minutes.

Add enough water to submerge the meat about 2/3 of the way (you may need more or less depending on the size and shape of your pot). Turn the heat to medium-high, cover the pot, and bring it to a boil. Once it's boiling, lower the heat and simmer, covered, for 2 hours. Every 30 minutes or so, uncover the pot and flip over the meat. This ensures that the marinade distributes evenly.
Turn off the heat and allow the meat to sit in the covered pot for about 2 hours, turning once again halfway through the cooling process.
Then allow the meat to cool completely in the refrigerator, preferably overnight. Take it out of the pot, slice it thinly, and garnish with sesame seeds and scallions.
processed_instructions: Trim the excess fat off the 2 - 2 1/2 pounds of beef shank and cut it into two to three 1-pound chunks.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil and add the beef chunks.
Boil the beef for about 8 minutes to remove impurities.
Drain the water and rinse the beef thoroughly under clean water.
Place the boiled beef shanks in a small, deep saucepan over medium-low heat.
Add the 1/2 cup of Shaoxing wine, 4 tablespoons of soy sauce, 3 tablespoons of dark soy sauce, 7 pieces of star anise, 1 sliced red pepper, 1 tablespoon of sugar, and 1 cinnamon stick to the pot.
Roll the meat around in the sauce for a few minutes to ensure all pieces are evenly coated.
Add enough water to submerge the meat about two-thirds of the way. Adjust based on the size and shape of your pot.
Turn the heat to medium-high, cover the pot, and bring the liquid to a boil.
Once boiling, lower the heat to a gentle simmer and cover the pot.
Simmer for 2 hours, uncovering the pot every 30 minutes to flip the meat over so the marinade distributes evenly.
Turn off the heat and leave the meat in the covered pot for about 2 hours to continue absorbing the flavors.
Flip the meat once more halfway through the 2-hour resting period.
Transfer the pot to the refrigerator and allow the meat to cool completely, preferably overnight.
Remove the beef from the pot and slice it thinly against the grain.
Arrange the slices on a serving plate and garnish with sesame seeds and sliced scallions.

Notably: Step 1 (trim, cut, boil, drain, rinse) split into steps 1-4; step 4 (add water, boil, lower, simmer with flipping) split into steps 8-11; step 5 (rest and flip) split into steps 12-13; step 6 (cool, slice, garnish) split into steps 14-16.

## ISSUES REMAINING:

- **glutenFree: True is INCORRECT and potentially dangerous**: Shaoxing wine contains wheat, soy sauce (standard) contains wheat, and dark soy sauce contains wheat. This recipe is definitively **not** gluten-free. If gluten-free substitutions are intended, the recipe must include notes specifying tamari instead of soy sauce, gluten-free dark soy sauce, and a substitute for Shaoxing wine (e.g., dry sherry). This is a critical safety error for anyone with celiac disease or gluten sensitivity.
- **No gluten-free substitution notes included**: Per review criteria, gluten-free substitutions should be noted. None are present despite the recipe being flagged glutenFree.

Needs clarification against the original author's intent (likely 1 dried red chili pepper, sliced).

The agent kept "sugar" but rock sugar has a different flavor profile and melting characteristic important in Chinese braising. A note should mention rock sugar as preferred.

title: Cornbread
original_instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Grease an 8-inch cast iron skillet or square baking pan with butter.
In a large bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the milk, melted butter, egg, and honey.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined — do not overmix. A few small lumps are fine.
Pour the batter into the prepared skillet. Bake for 20-25 minutes until the top is golden and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Let cool 5 minutes before cutting into wedges. Serve warm with butter and honey.
processed_instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Grease an 8-inch cast iron skillet or square baking pan with butter and set aside.
Whisk together 1 cup of yellow cornmeal, 1 cup of gluten free all-purpose flour, 1/4 cup of granulated sugar, 1 tablespoon of baking powder, and 1/2 teaspoon of salt in a large bowl.
In a separate bowl, whisk together 1 cup of milk, 1/3 cup of melted unsalted butter, 1 large egg, and 2 tablespoons of honey (if using) until well combined.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir gently until just combined. Do not overmix — a few small lumps are fine.
Pour the batter into the prepared skillet and spread it evenly.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until the top is golden and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before cutting into wedges.
Serve warm with butter and honey.

Original steps 2-3 had bare references like "the cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt."

- **Beginner-friendly breakdown**: Original 6 steps expanded to 9 steps. Step 1 (preheat + grease) split into steps 1-2; step 5 (pour + bake) split into steps 6-7; step 6 (cool + serve) split into steps 8-9.
- **Helpful beginner details added**: "spread it evenly" (step 6); "Remove from the oven" (step 8) makes the sequence explicit; "(if using)" added for optional honey (step 4).
- **Gluten-free substitution applied**: "All-Purpose Flour" changed to "gluten free all-purpose flour" in both ingredients and instructions.
- **Capitalization normalized**: Inconsistent casing standardized throughout (e.g., "Yellow Cornmeal" → "yellow cornmeal," "Granulated Sugar" → "granulated sugar," "Unsalted Butter" → "unsalted butter").
- **Ingredient format**: Fractions properly maintained (1/4, 1/2, 1/3) — already correct in original but consistently preserved.

## ISSUES REMAINING:

- **glutenFree: True is INCORRECT**: While the flour was substituted to gluten-free, **standard baking powder often contains wheat starch** (though many modern brands are GF). More critically, the recipe does not include a note advising users to verify their baking powder is gluten-free. Additionally, **yellow cornmeal** can be cross-contaminated with wheat depending on the brand/facility. A gluten-free note is essential — none is provided.
- **No gluten-free substitution note**: Per review criteria, gluten-free substitutions should be documented in a notes section. The agent silently changed the flour without any note explaining the substitution or advising that the original recipe calls for regular all-purpose flour. Users should be informed that texture/results may differ with GF flour, and a specific GF flour blend recommendation (e.g., Bob's Red Mill 1-to-1) would be helpful.

- **No note about cornmeal texture options**: For a beginner-friendly recipe, a note mentioning that fine-ground vs. medium-ground cornmeal affects texture would be helpful but is not critical.

## QUALITY SCORE: 6/10

The agent made clean, effective structural improvements — good step breakdown, all quantities embedded, proper imperative formatting, and consistent styling. The gluten-free flour substitution was applied correctly in both ingredients and instructions. However, the **isQuick: False flag is objectively wrong** for a 30-minute recipe, **isBalanced: True is dubious** for what is essentially a carb-heavy quick bread, and the complete absence of a gluten-free substitution note undermines the GF flag's reliability. title: Crying Tiger Thai Grilled Beef
original_instructions: Wash your meat with cold water, pat dry with paper towel, and place it in a plastic bag or shallow pan.
Mix the oyster sauce, fish sauce, soy sauce, garlic, sugar, and white pepper in a medium bowl until the sugar dissolves.
Coat the meat in the marinade on both sides. If your meat is thick, use a small knife to puncture a few holes to let the sauce seep through.
Pour the sauce over the meat in a ziplock bag or small pan, cover, and marinate in the refrigerator for 2-3 hours (or at least 30 minutes for a quick version, up to overnight).

While the meat marinates, make the Nam Jim Jaew dipping sauce. Heat a dry pan over medium-low heat. Add uncooked rice and toast, stirring often, until the grains turn golden brown and fragrant (about 5 minutes). Remove from heat, let cool completely, then grind into a coarse powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.

Finely chop the green onion, cilantro, and shallot and place them in a small mixing bowl. Warm the honey briefly in the microwave for 15-20 seconds until runny, let cool slightly, then add to the herbs and stir to combine.

Add fish sauce, fresh lime juice, water, tamarind concentrate (if using), and chili flakes to the herb mixture. Stir until evenly mixed. Taste and adjust with more lime juice, fish sauce, or water as needed. Stir in the toasted rice powder until fully incorporated. Set aside at room temperature.

Start the grill and cook the meat on medium-high heat. Cover the grill and cook until the meat is done to your preference — about 4-5 minutes per side for medium-rare (130°F internal), or 5-6 minutes per side for medium (140°F internal).
Remove from the grill and let the meat rest for 10-15 minutes on a cutting board before cutting to allow the juices to redistribute.

Cut the meat against the grain into thin strips. Serve with the Nam Jim Jaew dipping sauce, sticky rice, and fresh vegetables such as cabbage wedges, cucumber slices, and fresh herbs like Thai basil and mint.
processed_instructions: Wash the 1 1/4 - 1 1/2 pounds of steak with cold water and pat dry thoroughly with paper towels.

Pour the marinade over the steak and coat both sides evenly.
If the steak is thick, use a small knife to puncture a few holes on each side to help the marinade penetrate the meat.
Cover and refrigerate for 2-3 hours, or at least 30 minutes for a quick version, up to overnight.
Heat a dry pan over medium-low heat and add 2 tablespoons of uncooked jasmine rice.
Toast the rice, stirring often, until the grains turn golden brown and fragrant, about 5 minutes.
Remove the toasted rice from the heat and let it cool completely.
Grind the toasted rice into a coarse powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Set aside.
Finely chop the 2 stalks of green onion, 1/4 cup of cilantro, and 1/4 cup of shallot and place them in a small mixing bowl.
Warm the 4 tablespoons of honey briefly in the microwave for 15-20 seconds until runny, let cool slightly, then add to the herb mixture and stir to combine.

title: Easy Slow Cooker White Chicken Chili
original_instructions: Dice the onion and mince the garlic. Slice the jalapeńo lengthwise, scrape out the seeds with a spoon, then dice. Place the onion, garlic, jalapeño, and chicken breast in the bottom of a slow cooker.
Drain and rinse the cannellini beans and pinto beans, then add them to the slow cooker, along with the cumin, oregano, cayenne, and freshly ground black pepper. Pour the salsa over top.
Pour two cups of chicken broth over the contents in the cooker and then give everything a brief stir. Place the lid on the slow cooker and cook on high for four hours, or on low for eight hours.

After four hours on high or eight hours on low, remove the crockpot lid and carefully use two forks to shred the chicken meat (you can also remove it, shred it on a cutting board, and then return the meat to the slow cooker). Stir the chili, slightly mashing the beans with the back of the spoon as you stir. The mashed beans will help thicken the chili.
Serve the chili topped with your favorite ingredients, including shredded cheese, fresh cilantro, diced avocado, freshly squeezed lime, or tortilla chips (all optional).

Add the drained beans to the slow cooker.

Pour the entire 16 ounce jar of salsa verde over the top.

Stir the chili thoroughly, using the back of the spoon to slightly mash some of the beans as you stir. This will help thicken the chili.
Season with 1-2 teaspoons of salt to taste.
Ladle the chili into bowls and serve topped with your favorite toppings such as shredded cheese, fresh cilantro, diced avocado, a squeeze of fresh lime juice, or gluten free tortilla chips.

title: French Onion Soup
original_instructions: Caramelize onions: Add butter and onions to a large pot over medium heat. Season with a little salt and pepper and cook until softened. Add sugar and continue to gently stir until onions become golden brown and caramelized, about 25 minutes. Careful to not let them burn! Maintain a medium to medium-low heat.
Add the flour and cook for 3 more minutes. Add beef stock, wine, thyme, celery, and bay leaves. Cook partially covered for 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.

Toast bread: Arrange bread on a baking sheet and brush with melted butter on both sides. Bake for 15 minutes, flipping to the other side half way through. Remove from oven and rub a cut clove of garlic onto each piece of bread.

Broil: Turn oven to High broil. Remove bay leaves and the piece of celery from the soup and ladle soup into 4 oven proof bowls. Place the toasted bread on top of each bowl. Depending on the size of your bowls you may need two slices of bread. Sprinkle the tops generously with both types of cheese. Broil until the cheese is browned and bubbling.
processed_instructions: Add 3 tablespoons of butter to a large pot over medium heat and allow it to melt.
Add the 1 1/2 pounds of thinly sliced onions to the pot.
Season with 1/4 teaspoon of salt and a pinch of pepper, and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onions have softened.

Add 1/4 teaspoon of granulated sugar to the onions and continue to gently stir until the onions become golden brown and deeply caramelized, about 25 minutes total. Maintain medium to medium-low heat and be careful not to let them burn.
Sprinkle 2 tablespoons of gluten free all-purpose flour over the caramelized onions and stir to coat evenly.
Cook the flour with the onions for 3 more minutes, stirring frequently.
Add 6 cups of low-sodium beef broth, 1/2 cup of dry white wine, 1/2 teaspoon of thyme, 1 small chunk of celery, and 2 bay leaves to the pot.
Stir to combine, then partially cover the pot and cook for 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F while the soup simmers.
Arrange 8 slices of gluten free French bread on a baking sheet.
Brush both sides of each slice with the 4 tablespoons of melted butter.
Bake the bread for 15 minutes, flipping the slices over halfway through.
Remove the toasted bread from the oven and rub a cut side of a halved garlic clove onto each piece of bread.
Turn the oven to high broil.
Remove the 2 bay leaves and the chunk of celery from the soup and discard.
Ladle the soup into 4 oven-proof bowls.
Place 1-2 slices of garlic-rubbed toasted bread on top of each bowl of soup, depending on the size of your bowls.
Sprinkle the tops generously with the 1 cup of grated Gruyere cheese and 1/3 cup of grated Parmesan cheese, dividing evenly among the bowls.
Place the bowls on a baking sheet and broil until the cheese is browned and bubbling, about 3-5 minutes. Watch carefully to prevent burning.
Carefully remove the bowls from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving, as they will be extremely hot.

title: Pulled BBQ Chicken Sandwiches with Creamy Classic Coleslaw
original_instructions: Begin by preparing the coleslaw first, as it needs time to chill: In a small bowl, whisk together 1/3 cup mayonnaise, 1 tablespoon apple cider vinegar, 1 tablespoon sugar, 1/2 teaspoon celery seed, salt, and pepper until smooth.

In a large bowl, combine 3 cups shredded cabbage and 1 cup shredded carrots. Pour the dressing over the cabbage mixture and toss until evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes (or while the chicken cooks) to allow the flavors to meld together.

**For the BBQ Pulled Chicken:** In the slow cooker, combine 1 cup BBQ sauce, 1/2 cup chicken broth, 2 tablespoons brown sugar, 1 tablespoon apple cider vinegar, 1 teaspoon smoked paprika, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder, and 1/2 teaspoon onion powder. Stir well to combine all sauce ingredients.

Place 3 pounds boneless skinless chicken breasts in the slow cooker and turn to coat evenly with the sauce. Cover and cook on low for 6-8 hours or high for 3-4 hours until the chicken is tender and easy to shred.

When the chicken is cooked through and tender, remove it from the slow cooker and shred it using two forks. Return the shredded chicken to the slow cooker and mix it into the sauce. Let it warm in the sauce for an additional 10 minutes to absorb the flavors.
While the chicken is absorbing the sauce in its final minutes, check the coleslaw and adjust seasoning if needed. Remove it from the refrigerator to take the chill off slightly.
Toast the sandwich buns if desired for 1-2 minutes until lightly golden.

**To Serve:** Assemble your sandwiches by placing a generous amount of the BBQ pulled chicken on the bottom half of each sandwich bun. Top with a portion of the prepared coleslaw and add pickle slices if desired. Cover with the top half of the bun and serve immediately.

title: Quick & Authentic Chicken Tikka Masala with Basmati Rice & Naan
original_instructions: Preheat your oven to 400°F for the naan bread.
Cut 1 lb boneless skinless chicken breasts into 1-inch pieces and season lightly with salt and pepper.
Slice 1 small yellow onion into medium-sized pieces.
Remove the package of frozen Basmati rice from the freezer to slightly thaw while preparing the chicken.
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook for 2–3 minutes, or until it starts to soften.
Stir in 2 cloves minced garlic and 1 tablespoon minced ginger (or 1 teaspoon ginger paste), cooking for about 1 minute—just until fragrant (avoid burning).
Add the seasoned chicken pieces and sauté for another 3–4 minutes, browning them on all sides.

Pour the Tikka Masala sauce over the chicken, stir well to coat, then reduce the heat to medium-low. Cover and simmer for about 8–10 minutes, or until the chicken is fully cooked (internal temp 165°F/74°C). If the sauce appears too thick, add a small splash of water.
While the chicken is simmering, remove frozen naan bread from packaging and place it in the preheated oven for 4-5 minutes until warm and soft.
For the Rice: Transfer the frozen Basmati rice to a microwave-safe bowl, cover, and heat according to package instructions (usually 3-4 minutes).
For Enhanced Naan (optional): Mix 1-2 tablespoons melted butter with 1 clove minced garlic. When the naan is warm, brush with the garlic butter mixture and sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
Fluff the rice with a fork before serving.
Serve the chicken tikka masala over the basmati rice with warm naan bread on the side.

title: Red Wine-Braised Short Ribs with Buttery Garlic Mashed Potatoes
original_instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F.
Season 8 whole beef short ribs generously with salt and pepper, then coat all sides with 1/4 cup all-purpose flour.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add 1 diced yellow onion and 3 chopped carrots, cooking for a few minutes while stirring constantly.
Remove the vegetables to a plate and set aside.
Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil to the pot and increase heat to high.
Brown the ribs on all sides, about 45 seconds per side, then remove to a plate.
Pour 1 cup of red wine into the hot pot to deglaze, scraping the bottom to release any browned bits of flavor.
Bring the wine to a boil and cook for 2 minutes to reduce slightly.
Add 2 1/2 cups beef broth, salt, pepper, and 2 tablespoons of tomato paste, stirring to combine.
Taste the broth and adjust seasonings as needed.
Return the ribs and vegetables to the pot, then add 2 whole sprigs of thyme and 2 whole sprigs of rosemary.
Cover with the lid and transfer to the preheated oven.
Cook at 350 degrees F for 2 hours. **For the Mashed Potatoes:**
While the short ribs are cooking, cut 5 medium potatoes in half and place in a large pot with about 4 inches of water.
Cover and cook the potatoes until fork tender, about 15-20 minutes, timing this to finish about 45 minutes before the short ribs are done.
Drain water and leave potatoes in the pot, covered for 5 minutes to dry out.
Add 1/3 cup butter, 1 teaspoon chicken bouillon (if using), 1/2 cup milk, 1/4 cup sour cream (if using), salt, and pepper to the pot.
Mash with a potato masher until creamy and smooth, adding more milk (up to 3/4 cup total) if needed.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt and pepper if needed.
Cover the mashed potatoes to keep warm while the short ribs finish cooking. **Back to the Short Ribs:**
After the initial 2 hours of cooking, reduce oven heat to 325 degrees F and cook the short ribs for an additional 30 to 45 minutes until they are very tender, nearly falling off the bone.
Remove from the oven and let rest for 20 minutes with the lid on.
Skim off any fat that has risen to the top of the liquid.
Serve the ribs over the mashed potatoes with a spoonful of the rich gravy from the pot.

title: Slow Cooker Chicken Tikka Masala
original_instructions: Make the sauce: In a medium bowl, whisk together 2 tablespoons olive oil, 2 cloves minced garlic, 1 tablespoon minced fresh ginger, 1/4 diced yellow onion, 3 teaspoons garam masala, 1/2 teaspoon ground cumin, and 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper. Stir in 2 cans (8 oz each) tomato sauce and 1 can (15 oz) diced tomatoes until well combined.
Prep the chicken: Place 2 pounds chicken thighs in the slow cooker in a single layer. Season with 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon black pepper.
Add the sauce: Pour the prepared sauce evenly over the chicken thighs, making sure they are well coated. Cover the slow cooker with the lid.
Slow cook: Cook on LOW for 6-7 hours, or until the chicken is very tender and falls apart easily. Do not cook on HIGH as the sauce may scorch.

Shred and finish: Remove the chicken thighs and shred with two forks, discarding any bones. Return the shredded chicken to the slow cooker. Stir in 1 cup coconut milk until the sauce is creamy and well combined. Let warm through for 5 minutes.
Serve: Ladle the tikka masala over 4 cups hot cooked basmati rice. Garnish with 1/4 cup fresh chopped cilantro. Serve with warm naan bread on the side.

Stir in 2 cans (8 ounces each) of tomato sauce and 1 can (15 ounces) of diced tomatoes until well combined. Set the sauce aside.
Place 2 pounds of chicken thighs in the slow cooker in a single layer.
Season the chicken with 1/2 teaspoon of salt and 1/4 teaspoon of black pepper.
Pour the prepared sauce evenly over the chicken thighs, making sure they are well coated.
Cover the slow cooker with the lid and cook on LOW for 6-7 hours, until the chicken is very tender and falls apart easily. Do not cook on HIGH as the sauce may scorch.

Return the shredded chicken to the slow cooker.
Stir in 1 cup of unsweetened coconut milk until the sauce is creamy and well combined.
Let the tikka masala warm through for 5 minutes with the lid off.
Prepare 4 cups of basmati rice according to package directions while the chicken finishes.
Warm the gluten free naan bread according to package directions, if serving.
Ladle the tikka masala over the hot cooked basmati rice on serving plates.
Garnish with 1/4 cup of fresh chopped cilantro and serve with warm naan bread on the side.

title: Slow Cooker Coconut Curry Lentils
original_instructions: Dice the onion and mince the garlic. Peel the sweet potato and carrots. Dice the sweet potato (1/4-1/2 inch cubes) and slice the carrots.

Add the onion, garlic, sweet potato, carrots, lentils, curry powder, cloves, diced tomatoes, tomato sauce, and vegetable broth to the slow cooker. Stir to combine. Place the lid on the slow cooker and cook on high for 4 hours or low for 7-8 hours. Once cooked, the lentils should be tender and most of the liquid should be absorbed.

Stir the can of coconut milk into the lentils. Taste and adjust the salt or other spices as needed (the amount of salt needed will depend on the type of broth used and the salt content of the canned tomatoes).
To serve, add 1 cup cooked rice to a bowl followed by 1 cup of the lentil mixture. Top with finely diced red onion and fresh cilantro.

Peel the sweet potato and cut it into 1/4 to 1/2 inch cubes.
Peel the 2 carrots and slice them into rounds.
Add the diced onion, minced garlic, cubed sweet potato, sliced carrots, 2 cups of brown lentils, 3 tablespoons of curry powder, and 1/4 teaspoon of ground cloves (if using) to the slow cooker.
Add 1 can (15 ounces) of diced tomatoes, 1 can (15 ounces) of tomato sauce, and 3 cups of vegetable broth to the slow cooker.
Stir all the ingredients together until well combined.
Place the lid on the slow cooker and cook on high for 4 hours, or on low for 7-8 hours, until the lentils are tender and most of the liquid has been absorbed.
Stir 1 can (14 ounces) of full fat coconut milk into the cooked lentil mixture until fully incorporated.
Taste and adjust with 1-2 teaspoons of salt or additional spices as needed.
Finely dice the 1/2 red onion and chop the fresh cilantro or green onions for topping.
Spoon 1 cup of cooked rice into each serving bowl.
Ladle approximately 1 cup of the coconut curry lentil mixture over the rice.
Top each bowl with finely diced red onion and fresh cilantro or green onions and serve.

title: Smoky Spanish Chorizo & Lentil Stew with Crisp Romaine Salad
original_instructions: Begin by preparing all your vegetables: finely dice 1 medium yellow onion, dice 1 red bell pepper, thinly slice 1 carrot for the stew, slice 2 celery stalks, roughly mince 4 cloves of garlic, and cut 2 small Yukon Gold potatoes into ½ inch pieces.
Slice the 8oz Spanish chorizo into ¼ inch thick rounds.
Rinse 1 cup of dry brown lentils under cold water and set aside.
Wash 1 head of romaine lettuce and break into bite-sized pieces.
Chop 2 Roma tomatoes into small chunks.
Drain the small can of sliced olives. ## For the Main Dish: Chorizo Lentil Stew
Heat a large pot on medium heat. Add the chorizo slices in a single layer and cook for 1 minute on each side to render some fat. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate.
Add 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the pot. Add all the prepared vegetables (onion, red bell pepper, carrot, and celery - except the potatoes) and sauté for 5 minutes.
Add the 4 cloves of minced garlic, 1 teaspoon smoked paprika, and ½ teaspoon dried oregano and stir to combine.
Add ½ cup tomato sauce and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add the rinsed lentils, potato pieces, cooked chorizo, 6 cups vegetable broth, 1 tablespoon red wine vinegar, and 2 bay leaves.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the lentils are tender. ## For the Side Salad
While the stew is simmering, in a large bowl, combine the prepared romaine lettuce, chopped tomatoes, drained olives, and shredded carrots.
Just before serving, drizzle the salad with oil and vinegar dressing to taste.
Add a pinch of salt and toss to combine. ## Final Steps
When the lentils are tender, taste the stew and season with salt and pepper as needed.
Remove the bay leaves from the stew.
Serve the hot chorizo lentil stew in bowls garnished with some of the ½ cup chopped flat-leaf parsley.
Serve the tossed salad immediately alongside the stew for a refreshing contrast.

Ladle the hot chorizo lentil stew into bowls and garnish with the chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley.

If using 1/2 pound ground beef, heat a large pan over medium heat and cook the beef, breaking it into small pieces as it browns. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Once cooked, drain excess fat and set aside.

In the same pan (or a clean one if not using beef), heat 3 tablespoons olive oil over medium heat. Add the 3 cloves minced garlic and 1/4 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes (if using). Cook for about 30 seconds, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Add 1 28-ounce can of whole peeled tomatoes and 2 tablespoons tomato paste to the pan. Crush the tomatoes with a spoon and mix with the other ingredients.
Bring the sauce to a low simmer. If using ground beef, add it back to the sauce. Simmer for 5-10 minutes to develop flavor.
When the pot of water is boiling, add salt and cook 1 pound penne rigate according to package instructions, usually 8-10 minutes for al dente pasta.

While the pasta and sauce are cooking, prepare the salad. In a large salad bowl, combine 6 cups mixed greens, 1 cup halved cherry tomatoes, 1/2 cup sliced cucumbers, 1/4 cup thinly sliced red onions, and 1/2 cup shredded carrots.
When the pasta is done cooking, drain it well but do not rinse.
Remove the sauce from heat. Add the chopped 6 fresh basil leaves and the drained pasta to the pan with the sauce. Toss gently to coat the pasta evenly.
For the Salad: Sprinkle the prepared salad with 1/4 cup crumbled queso fresco if desired. Set aside with dressing of choice on the side.
Back to the Main Dish: Transfer the pasta to a serving dish and top with 1/2 cup freshly grated Parmesan cheese and the chopped 1/3 cup parsley.
Serve the pasta hot alongside the garden salad. Just before eating the salad, drizzle with your favorite dressing and toss gently to combine.

## ISSUES REMAINING:

More critically, **commercial dressing** ("dressing of choice") could easily contain gluten. **Canned whole peeled tomatoes** and **tomato paste** may contain gluten additives. No GF notes advise users to verify these items. Most importantly, simply substituting GF penne does not address the fundamental character of the dish — Penne Arrabbiata is an Italian pasta dish, and suggesting GF penne without noting that texture and cooking behavior differ significantly is unhelpful.

- **"Dressing of choice" — no quantity or GF note**: Listed without amount and without guidance. For a GF-flagged recipe, users need to be told to verify dressing is GF. Should also suggest a quantity (e.g., "2-3 tablespoons").

## QUALITY SCORE: 5/10

The structural processing is competent — the 20-step breakdown is logical, quantities are mostly consistently embedded, beginner details are thoughtfully added, and the ingredient list improvements are solid. The cuisineType, isBalanced, and isGourmet flags are all correctly set — a positive trend. However, **isQuick: False is wrong** for a 30-minute pasta dish, **glutenFree: True is unreliable** without addressing GF pasta cooking differences, commercial dressing verification, and Parmesan sourcing. title: Stuffed Shells
original_instructions: Preheat oven to 375 degree F. Spray a 9x13-inch baking dish with nonstick cooking spray. Set aside.

Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add pasta shells and cook until al dente, according to package instructions. Don't overcook the shells. Drain, and rinse with run cold water to stop cooking. Set the shells aside.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped spinach and sauté until wilted. Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
In a large bowl, combine ricotta cheese, egg, spinach mixture, 1 cup of the mozzarella cheese, Parmesan cheese, Italian seasoning, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper.
Spread 1 1/2 cups of the marinara sauce on the bottom of the baking dish.
Use a spoon to fill the pasta shells with the cheese and spinach mixture. Place in the baking dish.
Pour the remaining marinara sauce over the shells and sprinkle with remaining mozzarella cheese.
Cover with foil and bake for 30 minutes. Carefully remove the foil and broil for an additional 2 minutes to lightly brown the cheese.
Remove from the oven and garnish with fresh basil. Serve warm.
processed_instructions: Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Spray a 9x13-inch baking dish with nonstick cooking spray and set aside.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
Add the 20 jumbo gluten free pasta shells and cook until al dente according to package instructions. Be careful not to overcook the shells.
Drain the shells and rinse with cold water to stop the cooking process. Set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the 4 cups of chopped fresh spinach to the skillet and sauté until wilted, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the 3 minced garlic cloves and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Remove the spinach mixture from the heat and set aside.

Combine 15 ounces of ricotta cheese, 1 large egg, the sautéed spinach mixture, 1 cup of the shredded mozzarella cheese, 1/2 cup of grated Parmesan cheese, 1 teaspoon of Italian seasoning, 1/4 teaspoon of crushed red pepper flakes, 1/2 teaspoon of kosher salt, and 1/4 teaspoon of black pepper in a large bowl. Stir until well combined.
Spread 1 1/2 cups of the tomato sauce evenly across the bottom of the prepared baking dish.
Use a spoon to fill each cooked pasta shell generously with the cheese and spinach mixture.
Place the filled shells open-side up in the baking dish on top of the sauce.
Pour the remaining 1 1/2 cups of tomato sauce evenly over the stuffed shells.
Sprinkle the remaining 2 cups of shredded mozzarella cheese over the top.
Cover the baking dish tightly with aluminum foil.
Bake for 30 minutes until the sauce is bubbling and the cheese is melted.
Carefully remove the foil and switch the oven to broil on high.
Broil for an additional 2 minutes until the cheese is lightly browned and bubbly. Watch closely to prevent burning.
Remove from the oven and garnish with 2 tablespoons of chopped fresh basil leaves.
Let the stuffed shells cool for a few minutes before serving warm.

title: Turkey Sweet Potato Chili
original_instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the onion and red bell pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 4 to 5 minutes. Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, just until fragrant.
Add the ground turkey and cook, breaking it up with a wooden spoon, until browned and cooked through, about 5 to 7 minutes. Season lightly with salt and pepper.
Stir in 2 tablespoons of the chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, and oregano. Cook for 1 minute to toast the spices.
Add the fire-roasted tomatoes (with their juices), beans, sweet potato, and broth. Stir to combine.

Bring the chili to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sweet potatoes are tender and the chili has thickened. Taste and add additional chili powder, if desired. We usually do 2 1/2 tablespoons. Season with additional salt, if necessary.
Serve warm with your favorite toppings, such as shredded cheese, green onions, sour cream or Greek yogurt, jalapeños, or avocado.
processed_instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped yellow onion and diced red bell pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 4-5 minutes.
Add the 3 minced garlic cloves and cook for 1 minute, just until fragrant.
Add 1 pound of ground turkey to the pot and cook, breaking it up with a wooden spoon, until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Season the turkey lightly with kosher salt and black pepper.
Stir in 2 tablespoons of chili powder, 1 1/2 teaspoons of ground cumin, 1 teaspoon of smoked paprika, and 1/4 teaspoon of dried oregano.
Cook the spices for 1 minute, stirring constantly, to toast them and develop their flavor.
Add the 2 cans of fire-roasted diced tomatoes with their juices, 1 can of rinsed and drained kidney beans, the diced sweet potato, and 1 cup of chicken broth to the pot.
Stir everything together until well combined.
Bring the chili to a boil, then reduce the heat to low.
Cover and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sweet potatoes are fork-tender and the chili has thickened.
Taste the chili and add additional chili powder if desired, up to 3 tablespoons total. Season with additional salt as needed.
Ladle the chili into bowls and serve warm with your favorite toppings such as shredded cheese, thinly sliced green onions, sour cream or Greek yogurt, jalapeño slices, or avocado.

title: Zesty Lemon Chicken Pasta Dinner with Classic Caesar Salad & Artisan Bread
original_instructions: Begin by preparing the Caesar dressing: In a small bowl, combine 3 minced garlic cloves, 3/4 cup mayonnaise, 2 tablespoons grated Parmesan cheese, 5 minced anchovy fillets, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce, and 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard. Season with salt and pepper to taste and refrigerate until ready to use.
Preheat oven to 350°F for the bread (if planning to warm it).

Prepare the croutons: Heat 1/4 cup olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add 3 quartered garlic cloves and cook until brown, then remove garlic. Add 4 cups cubed day-old bread to the flavored oil and cook, turning frequently, until lightly browned. Transfer to a plate and season with salt and pepper.
Fill a large pot with water for the pasta, add salt, and bring to a boil.

While waiting for the water to boil, season 2-3 chicken breasts on both sides with salt, pepper, 1 teaspoon lemon zest, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/2 teaspoon Italian seasoning, and 1/2 teaspoon onion powder.
Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the seasoned chicken and cook for 3-5 minutes per side until cooked through (165°F internal temperature).
While the chicken is cooking, add 16 ounces (454g) farfalle pasta to the boiling water and cook according to package instructions until al dente.
If warming the artisan bread, place it in the preheated oven for 5 minutes for a crisp crust.
Remove the cooked chicken to a plate and tent with foil to keep warm while it rests.
In the same skillet used for chicken, add the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil. Once hot, add 1 chopped zucchini and 1 chopped yellow squash.
Season vegetables with salt, pepper, remaining 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder and remaining 1/2 teaspoon Italian seasoning. Cook on medium-high heat for 2 minutes.
Add 3 cloves minced garlic to the vegetables and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Drain the cooked pasta, reserving 1/4 cup of pasta water if needed for the sauce.
For the Caesar salad: Place 1 head of torn romaine lettuce in a large bowl.
Add the hot cooked pasta to the skillet with vegetables along with 4 tablespoons (57g) butter and 1/4 cup (60g) fresh lemon juice. Toss everything together until butter has completely melted.
Sprinkle 1 cup (95g) freshly grated Parmesan cheese over the pasta mixture and toss to combine.
Chop the rested chicken into bite-sized pieces and return to the pan with the pasta, mixing gently to incorporate.
Garnish the pasta with 1/4 cup (15g) fresh chopped parsley and additional Parmesan cheese if desired.
Back to the salad: Add the refrigerated dressing, remaining 4 tablespoons Parmesan cheese, and the prepared croutons to the romaine lettuce. Toss well to coat evenly.
Remove the artisan bread from the oven if warming, or serve at room temperature after slicing as needed.
Serve the lemon garlic chicken pasta alongside the Caesar salad and sliced artisan bread for a complete meal.

Fill a large pot with water, add salt, and bring to a boil.

While waiting for the water to boil, season 2-3 chicken breasts on both sides with salt, pepper, 1 teaspoon lemon zest, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/2 teaspoon Italian seasoning, and 1/2 teaspoon onion powder.
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the seasoned chicken breasts and cook for 3-5 minutes per side until cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 165°F.
While the chicken is cooking, add 16 ounces of gluten free farfalle pasta to the boiling water and cook according to package instructions until al dente.
Place the gluten free artisan bread in the preheated oven for 5 minutes to warm and crisp the crust.

Add the drained pasta to the skillet with the vegetables along with 4 tablespoons of butter and 1/4 cup of fresh lemon juice.
Toss everything together until the butter has completely melted and the pasta is evenly coated.
Sprinkle 1 cup of freshly grated Parmesan cheese over the pasta mixture and toss to combine. Add a splash of reserved pasta water if the sauce needs loosening.

Add the refrigerated Caesar dressing, the remaining 4 tablespoons of grated Parmesan cheese, and the prepared croutons to the lettuce.
